findings_added.category: writer-family R10 strict no-paraphrase EDITORIAL_CORRECTION violation

findings_added.scope: ig34_p0273_a037 LIST_ITEM Row 4 of PP-Examples Example 2 narrative

findings_added.v1_3_codification_candidate: v1.3 cut MUST add explicit EDITORIAL_CORRECTION verdict path: writer encounters PDF typo → writer choice = (a) preserve typo verbatim (R10 strict default) OR (b) flag with field `editorial_correction: true` + extracted_by.notes=`PDF original: pre-corrdinating` + repaired verbatim. Silent correction = R10 violation FAIL.

findings_added.category: writer-family NEW motif — Write-tool-overwrite silent data loss
findings_added.title: Sub-batch 28b initial dispatch reported atoms=342 but only persisted 50 atoms (p.280 only) to disk — Write tool overwrite-not-append silent data loss
findings_added.scope: Initial 28b dispatch via oh-my-claudecode:writer for p.276-280 (5 pages); 4/5 pages of atoms (p.276-279 ~290 atoms) lost without error signal; p.280 final-page atoms (50) survived as last-write-wins.
findings_added.rule_violation: v1.2 prompt §'工作模式' says 'Write tool 追加 JSONL 到 output_file' but Write tool semantically overwrites file content (not append). Writer subagent followed instruction literally → each per-page Write call replaced prior content → only last page survived. NEW writer-family motif distinct from wide-TABLE_HEADER family.
findings_added.repair_action: Option E full sub-batch rerun (cycle 1) — re-dispatched via oh-my-claudecode:executor with explicit Bash-heredoc append protocol (`cat << 'EOF' >> output_file`) per atom batch, plus `wc -l` verification after each page. Recovery: 224 atoms over p.276-280 clean. Rule B backup pdf_atoms_batch_28b.jsonl.pre-recovery-OptionE-p276-280.bak preserves the 50-atom partial as evidence.
findings_added.v1_3_codification_candidate: v1.3 cut MUST add procedural enforcement: writer/executor agents MUST use Bash-heredoc append (`cat << 'EOF' >> output_file`) for multi-page batches, NOT Write tool (overwrite default). v1.2 §'工作模式' wording '追加' is semantically correct intent but tool does not implement — wording must be made tool-faithful. Companion: post-page `wc -l` verification check.
findings_added.writer_family_motif: NEW writer-family motif (Write-overwrite vs Bash-append) — distinct from existing wide-TABLE_HEADER family (R10 strict paraphrase) and EDITORIAL_CORRECTION family (O-P1-88). Suggests systematic v1.2 spec-vs-tool gap.
findings_added.round_5_first_observation: First multi-session round where Write-overwrite motif manifested in initial 28b dispatch; oh-my-claudecode:writer specifically vulnerable; oh-my-claudecode:executor in 28a + 28b recovery completed correctly using Write tool (single per-batch consolidated Write to fresh file path, OR successive append via lower-level mechanism). Family-specific behavior pattern.

findings_added.category: v1.2 prompt spec ambiguity — atom_id page padding format inconsistency
findings_added.title: v1.2 prompt §'atom_id 命名规范' says '3 位 0-pad 页码' but example shows 4-digit `ig34_p0425_a012` and root pdf_atoms.jsonl uses 4-digit `ig34_p0001_a001` exclusively
findings_added.scope: ALL 148 28a atoms initially used 3-digit padding (p271, p272, p273...) per v1.2 spec text literal interpretation; 28b initial 50-atom write used same; 28b recovery via explicit prompt clarification used 4-digit padding (p0276...) correctly.
findings_added.rule_violation: v1.2 prompt §atom_id-命名规范 self-contradiction: text says '3 位' (3-digit) but example shows 4-digit (`p0425`). Root convention is 4-digit (verified `head -1 pdf_atoms.jsonl` returns `ig34_p0001_a001`).
findings_added.repair_action: Option H mechanical bulk fix (cycle 2) — Python regex `re.sub(r'"atom_id":"ig34_p(\d{3})_a', r'"atom_id":"ig34_p0\1_a', content)` applied to all 28a atoms; backup pdf_atoms_batch_28a.jsonl.pre-OptionH-atomid-padding.bak preserved. Post-fix: 372/372 atom_ids match `ig34_p\d{4}_a\d{3}`. 0 root collision.
findings_added.v1_3_codification_candidate: v1.3 cut MUST clarify: atom_id format = `<source_short>_p<NNNN>_a<NNN>` where page is **4-digit** zero-padded (NOT 3-digit per v1.2 text); rationale: pages range 1-577 (SDTMIG full doc) requires up to 3 leading zeros for max consistency. Update v1.2 text §atom_id 命名规范 + matching example. v1.3 add explicit regex `^[a-z]+_p\d{4}_a\d{3}$`.
findings_added.writer_family_motif: NOT writer-family error — both writer subagents followed v1.2 spec text literally (3-digit). v1.2 spec ambiguity is the root cause.

findings_added.category: NEW7 L6/L7 parent_section shortcut form — 'Example N' bare vs canonical full-form

findings_added.repair_action: DEFERRED to reconciler bulk-patch — affects ~30+ atoms in p.278-280 PP-relrec cluster; reconciler may apply mechanical patch (replace `"parent_section":"Example N"` → `"parent_section":"§6.3.5.9.3 Relating PP Records to PC Records — Example N"`) post-merge to root.
findings_added.v1_4_codification_candidate: v1.4 patch agenda: NEW7 chain spec parent_section canonicalization rule — 'Any atom whose immediate parent is a sub-Example/sub-Method HEADING (L6/L7 deep-nest) MUST use canonical full-form `§N.N.N — Sub-Heading-Title` not bare title'. Also covers cross-family Example structures (LB-Examples, IS-Examples, PP-Examples).
